chapter.title: Additional Topics
chapter.para: This chapter covers reusable components, accessibility, BPMN workflow diagrams, and the XSLT API reference.

section.title: Reusable components
section.para: SaxonForms contains several reusable template and function patterns that are used across controls, actions, and submissions. The class diagram below shows the logical structure of these runtime components.

section.title: Property resolution (get-properties mode)
section.para: The get-properties mode template (L637) is the central binding resolution mechanism. It accepts any XForms element and returns a map(*) containing the resolved nodeset, instance context, matching bind, and context model. This template is called by every control rendering template and every action template that needs to resolve a data reference.

section.title: Context instance resolution (get-context-instance-id mode)
section.para: The get-context-instance-id mode template (L561) determines which instance an XForms element refers to. It checks: (1) whether the element has a @bind that belongs to a specific model, (2) whether the element's nodeset expression references a specific instance via instance('id'), (3) whether the element's ancestor model has a default instance.

section.title: XPath evaluation functions
section.para: Two primary functions handle XPath evaluation against instance data:

listitem.para: xforms:evaluate-xpath-with-context-node() (L1448) — Evaluates an XPath expression with a given context node and namespace context. Used for binding resolution, calculate evaluation, and control value computation.
xforms:evaluate-xpath-with-instance-id() (L1495) — Resolves the instance root element first, then delegates to the context-node variant.

section.title: DOM field accessors
section.para: The get-field and set-field mode templates provide a uniform interface for reading from and writing to HTML form controls:

listitem.para: get-field — *:input (L3928), *:select (L3946), *:textarea (L3957).
set-field — *:input (L3969), *:select (L3989), *:textarea (L4004).

section.title: Refresh subsystem
section.para: The refresh phase uses four named templates that update different categories of DOM elements:

listitem.para: refreshOutputs-JS (L4345) — Re-evaluates and updates all registered xforms:output elements.
refreshRepeats-JS (L4462) — Regenerates repeat item content when instance data changes.
refreshRelevantFields-JS (L4550) — Shows/hides controls based on the relevant MIP.
refreshElementsUsingIndexFunction-JS (L4629) — Updates elements whose values depend on index() function results.

section.title: Accessibility
section.para: SaxonForms generates standard HTML form controls, which inherit the browser's native accessibility support. The following aspects are relevant:

section.title: Keyboard navigation
section.para: Since SaxonForms renders standard HTML <input>, <select>, <textarea>, and <button> elements, keyboard tab navigation works natively. The action-setfocus template (L7804) supports programmatic focus management, including suffixed-ID fallback and group→first-child focus delegation.

section.title: Labels and descriptions
section.para: The xforms:label template (L3054) renders labels associated with controls. xforms:hint (L2892) is matched but currently suppressed (empty template). The action-message template (L7631) handles xforms:message display via alert(), though model-level message dispatch has known issues (10.13.a, 8.2.2.a–c, 8.2.3.a–c).

section.title: CSS class hooks for state
section.para: The getHtmlClass template (L4175) computes CSS classes for controls based on MIP state: xforms-readonly, xforms-required, xforms-valid/xforms-invalid, xforms-enabled/xforms-disabled. These classes enable CSS-based visual feedback without additional JavaScript.

section.title: BPMN workflow diagrams
section.para: Workflow-level behavior is represented with BPMN 2.x artifacts under docs/bpmn. These diagrams document the development and validation processes rather than runtime behavior:

listitem.para: Development lifecycle workflow — Overall development process from feature request to release.
Regression gate workflow — Automated gate that validates W3C test suite pass rates and prevents regressions.
Git commit workflow — Pre-commit validation steps.
Test suite development workflow — Process for adding new W3C tests and fixture forms.
XSLT/XPath change workflow — Process for modifying XSLT source with regression verification.

section.title: XSLT API reference
section.para: This section provides a summary of the public API surface of SaxonForms — the named templates and functions that form the implementation contract.

listitem.para: xformsjs-main (L154) — Entry point. Parses XForms source, builds submission map, initiates model construction.
xforms-model-construct (L5205) — Initializes model instances and binds.
xforms-rebuild (L5296) — Processes bind elements, resolves nodesets.
xforms-recalculate (L5392) — Evaluates MIP expressions on all binds.
xforms-revalidate (L5531) — Validates required fields, constraints, XSD types.
xforms-refresh (L5622) — Updates DOM to reflect model state.
applyActions (L4666) — Main action dispatch entry point.

action-setvalue (L6782), action-insert (L7152), action-delete (L7388), action-toggle (L8005), action-dispatch (L7530), action-message (L7631), action-send (L7825), action-setindex (L7845), action-setfocus (L7804), action-reset (L7948), action-recalculate (L7913), action-refresh (L7927), action-script (L7741), action-load (L7764).
xforms:impose($xpath) (L24) — Rewrites XForms function calls to xforms: namespace.
xforms:resolve-index($xpath) (L95) — Substitutes index() calls with current values.
xforms:instance($id?) (L159/167) — Returns instance root element.
xforms:index($repeatId) (L123) — Returns current repeat index.
xforms:id($value, $node?) (L200/213) — XForms-compliant id() with xml:id and xsi:type support.
xforms:min($seq) (L270), xforms:max($seq) (L288) — NaN-safe min/max.
xforms:property($name) (L26) — Returns XForms property values (version, conformance-level).
xforms:boolean-from-string($s) (L43), xforms:count-non-empty($seq) (L54), xforms:power($base, $exp) (L65), xforms:choose($cond, $t, $f) (L78).
xforms:avg($seq) (L111), xforms:is-card-number($s) (L133), xforms:event($name) (L91).

Date/time: xforms:now() (L164), xforms:local-date() (L174), xforms:local-dateTime() (L184), xforms:days-from-date() (L195), xforms:days-to-date() (L223), xforms:seconds-from-dateTime() (L247), xforms:seconds-to-dateTime() (L276), xforms:seconds() (L302), xforms:months() (L332), xforms:adjust-dateTime-to-timezone() (L354/369).
Crypto: xforms:digest() (L382/390), xforms:hmac() (L405/414) — JS crypto.subtle bridge.
xsdh:canonical-type($type) (L16) — Normalizes XSD type QNames.
xsdh:is-type-valid($value, $type) (L41) — Validates value against XSD type.
Validate consistency between chapter workflows and existing BPMN process definitions.
Confirm accessibility guidance is backed by executable tests where available.
Verify API reference line numbers match current source.
Gather user review feedback before final full-document validation pass.
